behaviors.description: Request payload always includes the full taxonomy label list when calling the external API.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is request-builder -> API-client handoff (MOCK-MISS), not model judgment. An in-process fake API client that captures outbound payload can catch missing labels. REAL-INFRA is not required because behavior text is about payload content, not real network behavior. Rejected Agentic/System via behavior quote: "request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels" (plumbing assertion; excludes output-quality checks).
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module with a fake API transport, invoke classify(), and assert captured request contains every taxonomy label exactly once before send.

behaviors.description: Unknown provider label is mapped to "unknown" instead of being passed through.
behaviors.justification: Bug appears at API-response adapter -> normalization -> public return path boundary. A stubbed provider response with an out-of-taxonomy label reproduces the real failure without live infra. Rejected Agentic using quote: "When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns \"unknown\""; this scopes to deterministic guardrail logic, not correctness of model classification.
behaviors.test_description: Stub API to return label `medical` when taxonomy lacks it; call module entrypoint and assert final output is `unknown`.

behaviors.description: Normalization is deterministic for identical raw API responses.
behaviors.justification: This is a pure normalization invariant on one local transformation boundary (no I/O). MOCK-MISS check passes for Unit because behavior text explicitly isolates "identical raw API responses" and "normalization step" logic.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function repeatedly with the same raw response fixture (case/whitespace variants already fixed in fixture) and assert identical final label each run.

behaviors.description: Across repeated live calls, label stays consistent unless provider response changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires "repeated live API calls" and consistency relative to provider outputs; correctness depends on real model/service behavior under nondeterminism (LLM-DEP). A mock would hide provider variance and cannot validate this guarantee. Rejected Workflow because only one agentic decision per call is under test, not a multi-agent/multi-step chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real calls for same document+taxonomy, persist raw provider outputs and final labels; assert whenever two raw provider outputs are identical, normalized labels are identical, and flag unexpected drift patterns.

behaviors.description: Configured token budget is enforced before dispatch (truncate/reject per policy).
behaviors.justification: This validates module wiring between tokenizer/budget policy/request-dispatch gate (MOCK-MISS at local module boundary). Can be caught with deterministic in-process tokenizer + fake transport; no real OS/network needed. Rejected Agentic via quote: "enforces the configured token budget before dispatch" (infrastructure policy enforcement, not model output quality).
behaviors.test_description: Configure low budget, pass over-budget document, assert module either truncates or rejects per policy and that fake transport sees only compliant requests (or no request on reject).

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within configured budget.
behaviors.justification: Needs real provider token accounting on real AI API path; stubs can mask tokenizer/model-side differences (MOCK-HIDE). Even with deterministic assertion, execution depends on real AI API behavior, so default stays Agentic per LLM-DEP guidance. Rejected Workflow because this is a single-call property, not multi-agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real classification call(s) for representative document(s), read provider-reported token usage metadata, and assert usage <= configured per-call budget.

behaviors.description: On rate-limit responses, module waits and retries instead of surfacing immediate error.
behaviors.justification: Failure is retry-orchestration across API client, backoff policy, and caller contract. Deterministic stub returning rate-limit then success catches it without real network/subprocess. Rejected System/Agentic with quote: "API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries"; this targets control-flow plumbing, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use stub provider sequence: 429/RateLimit -> success; verify module delays per policy (fake clock acceptable), retries expected count, and returns successful label rather than error.
